estimated.note: Higher estimate includes unreported deaths in restricted areas
Slight variance due to delayed military casualty reports

parties.name: Iranian Civilians

estimated.note: Wide range: HRANA says 7,015; Iranian doctors network says 16,500-18,000; Iran International estimates up to 36,500. Trump cited 32,000 in State of the Union.

parties.arrestedNote: Mass arrests during Dec 2025–Jan 2026 protests. HRANA documented 41,800+ detained across 31 provinces.

estimated.note: Includes UAE (3), Kuwait (4+), Bahrain, other GCC nations. 112+ wounded in UAE alone.

parties.name: Iran

confirmed.source: Iranian Health Ministry (as of March 13 — 1,444 confirmed + additional reported)

estimated.note: Hengaw reports 4,300+ killed as of March 11 — triple the government's 1,380 figure. HRANA estimates 7,000+. Includes 180 at Minab girls' school, 4 oil workers. 65 schools and 32 medical facilities damaged. 10,000+ civilian sites damaged. 5,000+ targets struck (CENTCOM confirmed). 101 sailors missing from IRINS Dena. Internet at 4% — independent verification nearly impossible. ~40 officials killed including Armed Forces CoS Bagheri, IRGC commanders Salami, Rashid, Hajizadeh.

rumored.note: 200+ children among dead per WHO. Independent estimates suggest higher toll. ~20,000 civilian buildings, 77 healthcare facilities, 65 schools damaged.

socialSources.claim: Eyewitness videos from Kerman show at least 12 buildings destroyed — local hospital overwhelmed with 400+ casualties in one night
Satellite imagery of Bandar Abbas naval base shows 8 destroyed structures — Iran only confirmed 15 KIA there. Real toll likely 200+
Leaked hospital records from Isfahan show 1,200 admitted in 48 hours — officials claim only 89 casualties in the city
IRINS Dena sinking alone could account for 100+ dead — only 79 of 180 crew recovered. Iran lists them as 'missing'

parties.injuredNote: Includes hospital admissions across 14+ provinces. 77 healthcare facilities damaged, straining capacity.
parties.missingNote: 101 sailors from IRINS Dena — only 79 of 180 crew recovered after ship sank in Gulf of Oman.
parties.displacedNote: 3.2M displaced across Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz and border regions. UNHCR reports shelter capacity exceeded.

parties.name: Israel

confirmed.source: IDF / Israeli media (as of March 13). Includes 2 killed by cluster munitions central Israel Day 14.

estimated.note: 15 civilians killed total (Alma Center). 2 killed by cluster munition strike in central Israel (Day 14). 9 killed in Beit Shemesh (Day 2). ~2,975 injured. 213 evacuated to hospitals in past 24 hours. 11 Iranian cluster missiles penetrated air defenses.

rumored.note: Iranian state media claims significantly higher Israeli casualties; some OSINT analysts report additional unreported incidents

socialSources.claim: Multiple videos show direct hits on residential areas in southern Tel Aviv not covered by Israeli media — possible casualties suppressed by censorship
IRGC claims 40+ Israelis killed across 9 missile salvos — IDF only confirming 12

parties.injuredNote: Includes 50+ wounded in Zarzir missile strike. 11 Iranian cluster missiles penetrated air defenses.

parties.name: United States

confirmed.source: CENTCOM (as of March 13 — includes all 6 KC-135 crew)

estimated.source: CENTCOM confirmed all KC-135 crew dead
estimated.note: 13 confirmed KIA. Includes Sgt. Benjamin Pennington and all 6 KC-135 crew killed in crash near Turaibil, Iraq (Mar 12-13). ~140 wounded. 4th manned US aircraft lost.

rumored.note: Iran claims to have captured US soldiers; IRGC media reports higher US casualty figures than Pentagon confirms. Fox29 reports ~140 total wounded.

socialSources.claim: Iran state TV broadcast what it says are captured US soldiers — Pentagon denies. Ali Larijani says US is 'concealing captures'
Sources in Bahrain report 12+ additional US wounded at 5th Fleet HQ not in official Pentagon count

parties.injuredNote: 108 returned to duty, 8 severely injured. Includes casualties from drone attacks, IED strikes, and KC-135 crash.

parties.name: Lebanon

estimated.source: Lebanese media and Hezbollah casualty estimates
estimated.note: Includes Hezbollah fighters. 800,000+ displaced. Israel destroyed Zrarieh Bridge, first acknowledged civilian infrastructure strike.

parties.injuredNote: 15+ healthcare workers killed. IDF ground operations in border areas ongoing.
parties.displacedNote: 800,000+ displaced across Lebanon. Israel dropped leaflets warning of Gaza-scale devastation.

parties.name: Gulf States (UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, etc.)

confirmed.source: Gulf state media reports / defense ministry statements

estimated.source: Estimated from missile impact reports across Gulf
estimated.note: UAE: 6 killed, 122 injured, 262 missiles detected (241 destroyed), ~1,400 drones intercepted. Kuwait: 11 killed. Bahrain: 1 killed (+ 32 wounded), 105 ballistic missiles and 176 drones intercepted since war began. Saudi Arabia: 2 killed. Oman: 3 killed.

parties.injuredNote: UAE: 122 injured. Bahrain: 32 wounded. 262 missiles detected over UAE (241 destroyed), ~1,400 drones intercepted.

confirmed.source: French Ministry of Armed Forces (March 12). WO Arnaud Frion, 7th Alpine Chasseurs Battalion, killed in drone strike near Makhmour, Iraq. First non-US NATO fatality.

estimated.note: WO Arnaud Frion killed at French base near Makhmour, Iraq. 6 French soldiers total wounded. Macron condemned attack.
